verses.text: The king of Arad heard Israel was coming and fought against them, took some prisoners. Israel vowed: if Thou deliver them into our hand, we will utterly destroy their cities. The LORD hearkened, delivered up the Canaanites. They called the place Hormah — destruction. Guidance: A new generation's first battle. They lose ground, then vow — and the vow is heard. The wilderness taught them what their parents missed: ask God before you swing the sword. Hormah is the place where Israel's fear turned to faith.

They journeyed from Mount Hor to compass Edom; the soul was much discouraged. The people spake against God and Moses: no bread, no water — our soul loatheth this light bread. The LORD sent fiery serpents; many died. The people: we have sinned. The LORD: make a serpent of brass, set on a pole — every one bitten, looking on it, shall live. Guidance: They called manna 'light bread' — God's gift, despised. The cure for the bite was not antivenom; it was a LOOK. Salvation by glance — a foreshadow of another lifted-up One who said, look and live.

Israel journeyed — Oboth, Zared, the Arnon, then Beer. The LORD: gather the people, I will give them water. Israel sang: spring up, O well — the princes digged it, the nobles digged it with their staves. On to Mattanah, Pisgah top looking toward Jeshimon. Guidance: A song instead of a grumble. The same people who once cried for water now SING the well into rising. Faith learns to sing before the answer fully arrives — joy is what tunes the digging.

Israel sent to Sihon king of the Amorites: let us pass by the king's highway. Sihon refused, gathered his people, fought at Jahaz. Israel smote him with the sword, possessed his land from Arnon to Jabbok, took Heshbon, dwelt in the Amorite land. Guidance: Same request as Edom, same refusal — but God's verdict differs. The Amorite cup was full; the time of patience had ended. When the gate is finally shut from heaven, the wall comes down. Israel doesn't take what is asked; God gives what is owed.

Moses sent to spy out Jaazer; they took her villages, dispossessed the Amorites. They turned and went up by Bashan. Og king of Bashan came out against them. The LORD to Moses: fear him not, I have delivered him into thy hand. They smote him, his sons, all his people, possessed his land. Guidance: Og was a giant — a legend in his own time. God's answer: fear him not. The whisper before the battle decides the battle. Israel finishes east of the Jordan with two giant-kings beneath their feet.
